title: Restore, revert, reset and reflog deterministic misconception check

publication.changeSummary: Deterministic misconception item for Restore, revert, reset and reflog; blocked from formal exams until independent human review.

items.title: Restore, revert, reset and reflog: correct the failure model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this documented beginner mistake? Git undo is one universal command, and hard reset is the fastest safe way to reverse any mistake.

examEligibility.rationale: The deterministic answer and rubric are AI-assisted draft content awaiting independent human approval.

hints: Compare both options with the canonical model, declared boundary, and observable evidence in the lesson.

feedback.correct: Correct. Inspect working tree, index, commits, remotes, and reflog first; state what must survive, then choose the least destructive operation that changes only the intended layer.
feedback.incorrect: The selected statement preserves the documented misconception. Inspect working tree, index, commits, remotes, and reflog first; state what must survive, then choose the least destructive operation that changes only the intended layer.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction for Restore, revert, reset and reflog instead of the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the correction against the cited source and ensure the misconception option is plausible without reinforcing it.

options.text: Inspect working tree, index, commits, remotes, and reflog first; state what must survive, then choose the least destructive operation that changes only the intended layer.
Git undo is one universal command, and hard reset is the fastest safe way to reverse any mistake.

answer.explanation: Inspect working tree, index, commits, remotes, and reflog first; state what must survive, then choose the least destructive operation that changes only the intended layer.
